codeReview.summary: The Divergence Navigator implementation is solid and covers all requirements from the feature description and validation assertions VAL-DIV-001 through VAL-DIV-008 plus VAL-CROSS-P3-004. The code creates a 260px right-side panel within the Chart tab's flex layout, loads divergence entries from Schema 4C pairwise.{pair}.divergence_index[], renders a scrollable list with timestamp/primitive/TF/detection-status, color-codes entries by type (teal for A-only, amber for B-only, muted for Both), implements click-to-scroll via chart.timeScale().setVisibleRange(), provides primitive and session filter dropdowns (both defaulting to 'All'), shows count summary (total/A-only/B-only), handles empty divergence_index with 'No divergences' message, and ensures scrollability via overflow-y:auto on the entry list container. The code follows all project conventions: vanilla JS, no frameworks, global app state, CSS custom properties, CONFIG_COLORS palette from shared.js.

issues.description: Dead code: `hour < 0` in getSessionFromTime() is unreachable since parseInt of a 2-digit string can never be negative. The session classification logic still works correctly because the subsequent condition (hour >= 0 && hour < 2) catches hours 0-1. Cosmetic only.

Performance consideration: All divergence entries (handoff shows 9832 in the unfiltered view) are rendered into the DOM at once via innerHTML string concatenation. With very large datasets this could cause initial render latency. Not blocking since the worker verified scrolling works and the codebase has no virtualization libraries available (vanilla JS, no frameworks). Current approach is consistent with the project's technology constraints.

Minor: renderDivergencePanel() re-checks `if (_divEntries.length === 0 && app.evalData)` and re-calls loadDivergenceEntries() even though initDivergencePanel() already loaded entries. This is defensive but slightly redundant for the normal flow. No functional impact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature references VAL-DIV-009 in its fulfills list, but VAL-DIV-009 does not exist in the validation assertions document (build_plan/VALIDATION_ASSERTIONS_M2_ANALYTICS_AND_ANNOTATION.md only defines VAL-DIV-001 through VAL-DIV-008). This is a features.json metadata issue, not a code issue. The actual implementation covers all 8 defined assertions plus VAL-CROSS-P3-004.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json fulfills array includes 'VAL-DIV-009'; grep for VAL-DIV-009 across the repo returns no matches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the skill procedure correctly: read current state, wrote code following conventions, verified with agent-browser, and produced a complete handoff. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations. No skill updates needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback: { followedProcedure: true, deviations: [], suggestedChanges: [] }

summary: The divergence-navigator feature passes review. The implementation in site/js/divergence.js (359 lines) correctly loads Schema 4C divergence_index data, renders a scrollable color-coded list panel within the Chart tab, implements click-to-scroll, primitive and session filters, and count summary. All 8 defined VAL-DIV assertions plus VAL-CROSS-P3-004 are covered. Three non-blocking issues noted: dead code in session classification, potential DOM performance with very large datasets (acceptable given vanilla JS constraints), and a minor redundant data load check. The worker followed all project conventions (vanilla JS, CSS custom properties, global app state, CONFIG_COLORS palette) and the skill procedure.
